title: Site Quality Config
description: Per-site config for progressive-improvement-agent and competitor-research-agent. One YAML file per site.

base_url.description: Full URL with scheme. Defaults to https://<domain>.

what_we_do.description: One-paragraph description used in LLM prompts to ground the analysis.

seed_urls.description: Starting URLs (relative to base_url or absolute). Defaults to ['/']. Sitemap entries auto-included if discoverable.

path_excludes.description: Glob patterns of paths to skip (e.g., ['/admin/*', '/api/*']).

throttle_ms.description: Delay between requests to be polite.

render_js.description: If true, use a headless browser. Required for SPAs. Adds Playwright dependency.

auto_implement_threshold.description: Recs at confidence >= this and severity in {medium, high, critical} are tier=auto.

issue_categories.description: Override the default category list. Use to focus the agent on specific concerns (e.g., only 'incorrect-categorization' for a catalog site).

scan_mode.description: How to discover what we offer: 'website' (crawl public pages, default) or 'codebase' (scan a local repo for features). codebase mode is for frameworks, libraries, or apps without a public website.

codebase.description: Required if scan_mode=codebase. Tells the agent which repo + how to extract features.

repo_path.description: Absolute path to the repo to scan.

include_globs.description: File patterns to include (e.g. ['**/*.py', '**/*.md', 'README.md']). Falls back to README.md + top-level docs if unset.

feature_summary_files.description: Files that summarize features (README, ARCHITECTURE.md, etc.). These get full attention from the LLM.

competitors.description: Used by competitor-research-agent only. Optional for progressive-improvement.

seed_domains.description: Known competitor domains/projects. For website mode: domains to crawl. For codebase mode: domain or GitHub project name (e.g. 'n8n.io', 'temporal.io'). If empty, agent uses LLM to brainstorm.

auto_implement.description: Master toggle. When false, ALL recs require user reply. When true, tier=auto recs ship without confirmation. Until you've established a track record DO NOT enable this.

implementer.description: Optional: where to dispatch auto-tier recs. Required if auto_implement=true.

agent_id.description: Downstream agent to receive auto-tier recs.

allowed_paths.description: Glob patterns the implementer is allowed to MODIFY in this repo. When set, any rec whose target_files fall outside the allowlist is auto-deferred. Used to keep web-content agents away from mobile/, ios-extensions/, etc. Glob syntax via fnmatch — 'mobile/**' matches anything under mobile/. Empty / unset = no allowlist (legacy behaviour, can touch any path).

excluded_paths.description: Glob patterns the implementer must NOT modify, even if they pass allowed_paths. Applied AFTER allowed_paths. Use this to carve narrow holes in a broad allowlist.

post_apply.description: What to do after the implementer ships a commit. All keys default to true so legacy configs keep working.

kick_mobile_build.description: After an apply, kick off an EAS mobile build if any file under mobile/ was touched. Set false to require manual mobile builds even when scope rules permit mobile edits.

kick_backend_deploy.description: After an apply that touches backend (src/, db/, frontend/), kick off the Azure Container App deploy. Set false to require manual deploys.

scope_by_dispatch_kind.description: Optional. Per-dispatch_kind scope overrides. When the implementer is invoked with a dispatch_kind that matches a key here, the value REPLACES the default allowed_paths / excluded_paths / post_apply for that run. Typical use: let the crash-watcher (dispatch_kind=crash-fix) edit mobile/ while SEO / PI / catalog-audit (default dispatch_kind) stay in web+backend paths.

^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*$.description: Per-dispatch-kind scope. Same shape as the parent implementer block (allowed_paths, excluded_paths, post_apply).

subject_template.description: Defaults to '<agent-id> — {site} — {tag}'. Subject is auto-prefixed with [<agent-id>:<request-id>] for routing.

runs_root.description: Where per-run output dirs are created. Default: ~/.reusable-agents/<agent-id>/runs
